title: Unexpected error is reported in case when use 'sat' command with '-tempinduct-def' option
body:
I have an unexpected error when I use the 'sat' command with the '-tempinduct-def' option.
And I don't have this error when I use just '-tempinduct' option.

## Steps to reproduce the issue

test case:
```
module middle
(
	input x,
	input y,
	output o
);

assign o = x + y;
endmodule
```
script:
read_verilog test.v
proc
sat -prove x 1 -prove y 0 -tempinduct-def middle

## Expected behavior
3. Executing SAT pass (solving SAT problems in the circuit).

Setting up time step 1:
Final constraint equation: { } = { }
Imported 1 cells to SAT database.
Import proof-constraint: \x = 1'1
Import proof-constraint: \y = 1'0
Final proof equation: { \y \x } = 2'01

** Trying induction with length 1 **

Setting up time step 1:
Final constraint equation: { } = { }
Imported 1 cells to SAT database.
No constraints for initial state found.

Import proof-constraint: \x = 1'1
Import proof-constraint: \y = 1'0
Final proof equation: { \y \x } = 2'01

[base case 1] Solving problem with 11 variables and 22 clauses...




## Actual behavior

3. Executing SAT pass (solving SAT problems in the circuit).

Setting up time step 1:
Final constraint equation: { } = { }
Imported 1 cells to SAT database.
Import proof-constraint: \x = 1'1
Import proof-constraint: \y = 1'0
Final proof equation: { \y \x } = 2'01
ERROR: Assert `!undef_mode || model_undef' failed in ./kernel/satgen.h:90.
